title: oxalis acetosella subsp. oregana
intro: Oxalis acetosella subsp. oregana, also known as Oregana Wood Sorrel, is a subspecies of the Oxalis acetosella plant, belonging to the wood sorrel family and native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Oregana Wood Sorrel include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and support immune function. The herbal preparations of Oregana Wood Sorrel include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
